sourceMovie.title: Resident Evil
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Resident Evil works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Apocalypse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Resident Evil clicked for you, Resident Evil: Apocalypse (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Extinction
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Resident Evil clicked for you, Resident Evil: Extinction (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Logan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Resident Evil clicked for you, Logan (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Men in Black
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Resident Evil clicked for you, Men in Black (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Resident Evil?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil: Apocalypse, Resident Evil: Extinction, Extinction—each shares DNA with Resident Evil while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Resident Evil?
faq.answer: Resident Evil (2002) is best described as Horror, Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Resident Evil part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Resident Evil Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
